_description: Workbook queue. Each entry is one real-world agent APPLICATION (a reusable target), not a framework tutorial. `harness_focus` names the harness attribute the example is built to demonstrate -- that lens, not the framework, is the lesson. Pick the next pending item by priority and scaffold it with the 4-phase commit protocol in AGENTS.md.

_field_notes.domain: the business area the use case lives in
_field_notes.harness_focus: the harness attribute this example isolates (action space, observation shaping, structured output + validation, routing, guardrails, grounding, delegation, ...)
_field_notes.framework: advisory default only -- keep examples provider/framework-agnostic (configurable model, no hardcoded vendor in code). Known values in use: langgraph, openai-sdk, pydantic-ai, crewai, langchain-lcel, autogen.
_field_notes.group: supervisor-subagent cluster key -- links a supervisor to its subagents within a domain (omit for standalone agents)
_field_notes.role: standalone | supervisor | subagent -- standalone if no supervisor pattern; supervisor if it dispatches domain subagents; subagent if it runs under a named supervisor
_field_notes.supervisor_id: id of the parent supervisor entry (subagent entries only; omit for standalone and supervisor entries)
_field_notes.subagents: ordered list of subagent entry ids dispatched by this supervisor (supervisor entries only)
_field_notes.synthetic_data: what synthetic dataset to generate for testing and demos -- specific about volume, fields, and deliberate anomalies to inject
_field_notes.free_api: public API used for real-data grounding (null if none); includes auth requirements and usage limits
_field_notes.gradio_ready: true if this agent is a strong Gradio / Hugging Face Spaces demo candidate (clean I/O, visible output, safe for public)

synthetic_data_strategies.finance_accounting: Use Faker (Python) for company/vendor names, IBAN numbers, dates. Generate realistic 4-digit GAAP chart-of-accounts codes: 1xxx=assets, 2xxx=liabilities, 3xxx=equity, 4xxx=revenue, 5xxx=COGS, 6-8xxx=expenses. Include cost centres (CC1001-CC9999), profit centres, and business areas. Inject ~12-15% imbalance or miscoding rate for validation testing. Volume: 100-500 journal lines per scenario.
synthetic_data_strategies.manufacturing: Generate BOM structures with 3-4 levels using realistic part numbering (ASSY-001, COMP-001-A). Use IPC-standard defect codes (D001-D099). Shift codes: A (06:00-14:00), B (14:00-22:00), C (22:00-06:00). Line IDs: LINE-01 through LINE-08. Realistic OEE: world-class 85%+, good 70-85%, poor <60%. MTBF range: 200-2000 hours. Generate 8-52 weeks of weekly data for trend detection.
synthetic_data_strategies.inventory_warehouse: Generate 500-2000 SKU master records using UNSPSC product taxonomy (8-digit codes). Model demand with Poisson distribution (mean 10-200 units/week). Age distribution: 60% <30 days, 25% 30-90 days, 10% 90-180 days, 5% >180 days. Inject 10-15% slow-moving and 5% no-movement SKUs. Include ABC classification (A=top 10% by value, B=next 20%, C=remaining 70%).
synthetic_data_strategies.procurement: Use UNSPSC 8-digit commodity codes for taxonomy. Generate DUNS-format vendor IDs (9-digit numeric). Apply 80/20 rule: top 20% of suppliers represent 80% of spend. Include off-contract (maverick) spend at 15-20% of total. Generate 500-2000 PO lines. PPV range: -10% to +25% vs standard.
synthetic_data_strategies.logistics: Use real US metro area lat/lng coordinate pairs for OSRM routing calls. NMFC freight class codes: 50-500 (lower = denser freight). Generate shipment records with origin/destination zip codes, carrier, mode (FTL/LTL/parcel), weight, actual cost, planned cost, delivery date vs ETA. Inject 10-15% late deliveries and 8% short shipments.
synthetic_data_strategies.executive: Composite 13-week weekly KPI time series across 4-5 dimensions (financial, operational, customer, people). Include deliberate threshold breaches at weeks 8-10. Add trend reversal signals (3 weeks declining after 6 weeks improving). Use SMART KPI targets: specific numeric thresholds for red/amber/green status.
synthetic_data_strategies.security_ops: Generate a daily signal digest, not a single dataset: 15-20 SIEM-style auth events (impossible-travel, off-hours admin login), a dependency-scan delta (reuse the OSV.dev pattern from example 28), an IAM role-assignment diff (new grants, orphaned accounts), and an open incident ticket. Inject one cross-domain correlation deliberately (same identity appears in the anomalous login AND the orphaned-account flag) so the supervisor has a real pattern to surface, mirroring the Line-3 correlation in example 57's synthetic data.
synthetic_data_strategies.governance_controls: Compose from existing finance-accounting datasets rather than inventing new ones: reuse the journal-entry, bank-statement, and fixed-asset synthetic generators from examples 44/46/50, add a 15-role segregation-of-duties matrix with 4-6 deliberate toxic-combination conflicts, and a change log mapping every exception to a preparer/approver identity pair so the audit-trail subagent has citable resolution records to cite.

_data_sourcing_notes.gradio_deployment_notes: For Gradio/HF Spaces demos: (1) rate-limit external API calls with time.sleep(0.5) between requests; (2) cache API responses with functools.lru_cache or shelve for repeat demo inputs; (3) keep demo input sets small (<=10 records, <=3 suppliers, <=5 transactions); (4) use gr.Progress() for multi-step agent pipelines; (5) wrap model calls in try/except with gr.Error() for clean failure handling; (6) use OpenRouter as the model backend to leverage rate-limit headers and avoid OpenAI quota issues in public demos; (7) store API keys in HF Secrets, never in app code.
_data_sourcing_notes.openrouter_notes: For demos requiring a production-safe model backend: use OpenRouter (openrouter.ai) with the openai SDK pointed at https://openrouter.ai/api/v1. This enables model-string swapping without code changes, built-in rate limiting, and access to free-tier models for public demos. Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env. See examples 24, 25, 27, 41, 42 for the pattern.

_review_notes_2026_06_30._purpose: Findings from a critical pass over the pending queue (ids 44-89) plus the rationale for the new entries added below (ids 90-102). Kept here instead of only in chat so the next session reading this file has the reasoning, not just the result.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.data_integrity_fix: Ids 44-48 were still flagged status=pending despite being fully built and merged on disk (src/, main.py, README.md, workbook, demo.py all present for each). Corrected to status=done. The root README example table at the time of this review still does not list examples 44-48 either -- the AGENTS.md post-example protocol step ('docs(README): add example N to root table') appears to have been skipped for this whole batch and should be backfilled.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.framework_monoculture: Of 89 queue entries, 59 are framework=langgraph, 29 are openai-sdk, 1 is pydantic-ai. Zero use crewai, zero use plain langchain (LCEL without langgraph), zero use autogen/ag2, zero use the raw Anthropic SDK directly. The 46 pending items as written would not change this ratio -- they add new business domains on the same two frameworks. If the project's purpose is shifting toward training people across frameworks (per project direction discussed 2026-06-30), framework diversity is a bigger gap than domain coverage right now.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.harness_redundancy: Several pending items repeat an already-demonstrated harness lesson under a new domain noun rather than teaching something new: the 'agent calls a deterministic Python calculator, narrates the result' pattern appears at least 7 times (ids 50, 52, 58, 61, 62, 63, 64, 69) and the 'supervisor conditionally dispatches domain subagents and synthesises a dashboard' pattern appears 5 times near-identically (ids 57, 67, 74, 81, plus 87 one level up). Useful as a reference library, but low marginal teaching value past the second or third repetition -- new high-priority work should teach a genuinely new harness pattern, not a new formula.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.domain_gaps: 44 of 46 pending items sit in five adjacent operational domains (finance-accounting, manufacturing, procurement, inventory-warehouse, logistics). Security is entirely absent as a domain -- not one queue entry, done or pending, touches threat triage, access governance, incident response, or compliance evidence. 'Governance' has exactly one entry (id 12, done) and it is an executive-critique pattern (read a board pack, surface risks), not a controls/audit pattern. No entry models human-in-the-loop as an actual execution gate -- ids 45 and 47 mention 'approval tier routing' but only as a routing label, never as something that pauses execution pending a human decision.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.external_inspiration: github.com/msitarzewski/agency-agents was reviewed for role-taxonomy inspiration (not for code -- it is a library of Claude Code subagent persona prompts, already installed in this environment's ~/.claude/agents/, not standalone runnable Python agents). Its security/ division (10 roles: AppSec Engineer, Security Architect, Blockchain/Cloud/Compliance specialists, Incident Responder, Penetration Tester, SecOps, Threat Detection/Intel) and specialized/ division (CFO, Automation Governance Architect, Data Privacy Officer, Identity Graph Operator, Accounts Payable Agent) directly informed the new security-ops and governance-controls clusters added below as ids 91-99.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.new_entries_added: ids 90-102: id 90 is a standalone human-in-the-loop approval-gate pattern (interrupt/resume, not just a routing label) that ids 91-99 depend on. ids 91-96 are a Security Operations org (1 supervisor + 5 subagents) modeled on the agency-agents security division. ids 97-99 are a Ledger Integrity / Controls org that reuses the already-built ids 44/46/50/55 as subagents rather than duplicating them, plus two new internal-audit subagents (segregation-of-duties, audit-trail) that had no prior representation in the queue. ids 100-102 port the example-1 tool-calling task verbatim into crewai, langchain-lcel, and autogen so a learner can diff four frameworks solving the literal same problem.
_review_notes_2026_06_30.recommendation: Work ids 90, 91, 97, and 100-102 next, ahead of the remaining finance-accounting/manufacturing/procurement/inventory/logistics pending items, even though several of those are also marked priority=high. Existing high-priority items were left unmodified rather than silently demoted -- flagging for explicit confirmation: ids 61 and 72 are near-duplicate harness lessons of ids 52/58 and are reasonable candidates to demote to priority=low if queue bandwidth is constrained.

queue.harness_focus: aging-bucket segmentation + tone-calibrated escalation ladder + credit-hold flagging -- agent segments AR by days-outstanding buckets (current/1-30/31-60/61-90/90+), selects communication channel per bucket (email reminder / formal notice / final notice / legal referral), drafts a tone-calibrated collection communication that escalates appropriately per tier, and flags accounts for credit hold based on days overdue and amount outstanding
queue.description: Process an accounts receivable aging report to generate a prioritised collection action plan. Agent segments customers by overdue bucket, selects escalation tier, drafts a personalised collection letter per customer, identifies credit hold candidates, and outputs a structured CollectionPlan with letters and recommended next actions.
queue.synthetic_data: AR aging report with 50-80 customers: invoice number, invoice date, due date, outstanding amount, customer name, prior contact log (dates and methods). Distribution: 30% current, 25% 1-30 days, 20% 31-60 days, 15% 61-90 days, 10% 90+ days. Some customers with prior unanswered reminders to trigger escalation.

queue.harness_focus: DAG-ordered task orchestration + typed status state machine with dependency gates -- month-end close tasks form a directed acyclic graph; orchestrator dispatches tasks in dependency order, each task returns a typed CloseTaskStatus (pending/in-progress/blocked/completed/exception), downstream tasks are gated on upstream completion, blocked tasks surface a reason and estimated delay, orchestrator re-derives critical path after each status change
queue.description: Orchestrate a month-end financial close process defined as a dependency-ordered task list. Agent tracks which tasks are complete, identifies which are ready to start, dispatches next available tasks, handles blocked states, and maintains a live CloseStatus object showing overall progress, SLA risk, and critical path.
queue.synthetic_data: Close task list with 20-30 tasks across categories (subledger close, intercompany elimination, accruals posting, FX revaluation, consolidation, reporting). Each task has: owner, dependency list, SLA hours, current status. Inject blocked tasks and a critical path bottleneck to test orchestration logic.

queue.harness_focus: asset lifecycle state machine + deterministic depreciation schedule computation -- assets transition through acquire/capitalise/active/partially-disposed/fully-disposed/written-off states; agent computes straight-line and declining-balance depreciation schedules as deterministic Python (not LLM arithmetic), handles partial-year additions and mid-period disposals, outputs a structured AssetRegister with net book value and remaining useful life per asset
queue.description: Manage a fixed asset register: classify acquisition events, compute depreciation schedules (straight-line and declining balance) for each asset, handle disposals and write-offs, and produce a structured asset register with net book value, accumulated depreciation, and remaining useful life per asset.
queue.synthetic_data: Asset register with 50-100 assets: acquisition date, cost, useful life (years), residual value, depreciation method, class (plant/equipment/IT/vehicles/leasehold). Include 10% with partial-year mid-period additions, 15% with prior disposals, 5% write-offs. Compute over a 5-year history.

queue.harness_focus: multi-account FX aggregation via real ECB API + 13-week liquidity ladder + threshold alerting -- agent fetches live EUR-based FX rates from ECB API (no key), consolidates balances from N accounts in M currencies into a single reporting currency equivalent, constructs a 13-week cash flow forecast using scheduled receipts and payments, flags any week where projected balance falls below a configurable liquidity floor
queue.description: Build a daily treasury cash position: consolidate bank account balances across currencies using live ECB FX rates, layer in scheduled receipts and payments, produce a 13-week cash ladder, and alert on any period falling below the liquidity reserve threshold.
queue.synthetic_data: 5-8 bank accounts in 3-4 currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, SAR). Current balances and a 90-day schedule of expected receipts (customer payments, drawdowns) and payments (supplier, payroll, debt service). Inject at least one week where projected balance drops below threshold.
queue.free_api: European Central Bank FX Rate API (sdw-wsrest.ecb.europa.eu -- no key required, live EUR-based exchange rates for 40+ currencies)

queue.harness_focus: price-volume-mix variance decomposition (Python tool) + natural-language driver explanation -- agent receives budget and actual P&L by product and cost centre, calls a Python PVM decomposition tool that splits revenue variance into price effect, volume effect, and mix effect, then drafts natural-language explanations for each material variance citing the specific business driver
queue.description: Produce a financial variance analysis: decompose budget-vs-actual revenue and cost variances into price, volume, and mix components per product and cost centre, rank variances by materiality, and draft board-ready natural-language explanation for each top-10 variance citing the underlying business driver.
queue.synthetic_data: Budget P&L vs actual P&L for 3 months across 5-8 products and 4-6 cost centres. Include deliberate driver shifts: one product with +15% volume but -8% price, one cost centre with adverse labour rate variance, one product discontinued mid-period. Enough signal for a meaningful PVM decomposition.

queue.harness_focus: driver-based model construction + sensitivity sweep tool -- agent extracts key business drivers from a narrative brief, builds a linked 3-year P&L driven by those assumptions, calls a Python sensitivity-sweep tool that runs +-10%/+-20% on the top-5 drivers, and returns a SensitivityOutput with a tornado-ranked driver table
queue.description: Build a driver-based budget and 3-year forecast: extract planning assumptions from a business narrative, construct a linked P&L model, run multi-scenario sensitivity analysis on top-5 value drivers, and produce a structured forecast with tornado chart data for executive review.
queue.synthetic_data: Business planning narrative with embedded assumptions: revenue growth rate, average selling price, headcount plan, COGS%, capex budget. Prior 2-year actual P&L for anchoring. Generate 3 scenarios (base/bear/bull) with different driver assumptions.

queue.harness_focus: multi-audience structured fan-out with consistency validation -- same typed FinancialPeriodData fans out simultaneously to three specialist formatter agents (board narrative, operations dashboard JSON, investor KPI summary); each produces a different schema and aggregation level; a final consistency-check agent confirms no material discrepancy across outputs before returning all three
queue.description: Generate three versions of a monthly management report from a single financial dataset: a board-level narrative (1-page MD&A), an operations dashboard (JSON with KPIs and RAG status per business unit), and an investor-facing KPI summary (10 metrics with period-on-period movement). All three must be internally consistent.
queue.synthetic_data: Monthly management accounts: P&L by business unit, balance sheet snapshot, cash flow statement, 8-10 operational KPIs (revenue, EBITDA, headcount, NPS, OTIF, order intake). 3-month trend for each metric. Include at least 2 adverse variances requiring narrative explanation.

queue.harness_focus: multi-signal anomaly scoring + SAR narrative generation -- agent applies configurable rule checkers (velocity: >5 transactions same payee in 24h; round-number: amount is exact multiple of $1000 >$5k; geo-impossibility: location A and location B within 60 min; new-payee + large-amount flag) to a transaction batch, assigns composite fraud score per transaction, drafts a Suspicious Activity Report narrative for each transaction exceeding the alert threshold
queue.description: Screen a payment transaction batch for fraud signals. Agent evaluates each transaction against a configurable rule set, computes a composite fraud score, outputs a structured FraudAlert for each flagged transaction, and drafts a Suspicious Activity Report narrative suitable for compliance review.
queue.synthetic_data: 500-1000 payment transactions (date, time, payer, payee, amount, location, payment method). Embed anomaly patterns: 3 velocity clusters (same payee 6x in 12h), 5 round-number large amounts to new payees, 2 geographic impossibility sequences, 4 after-hours weekend wire transfers.

queue.harness_focus: multi-domain grounded Q&A with confidence attribution and escalation gate -- agent classifies executive query to its domain (finance/operations/HR/legal), retrieves from the matching knowledge partition, drafts an answer with source citation and a numeric confidence score, escalates to human expert when confidence falls below threshold rather than generating a low-confidence response
queue.description: C-suite Q&A agent: classify an executive question to its domain, retrieve relevant context from domain-partitioned knowledge bases (financial reports, operational KPIs, HR summaries, compliance tracker), draft a concise grounded answer with source citations and confidence score, and emit an escalation flag when the agent cannot answer with sufficient confidence.
queue.synthetic_data: Domain-partitioned corpus: (1) last 3 board packs + management accounts, (2) operational KPI exports, (3) HR headcount and turnover summaries, (4) compliance obligation register. Generate 20-30 diverse executive Q&A pairs with expected sources and acceptable confidence floors.

queue.harness_focus: hierarchical multi-domain orchestration + conditional subagent dispatch -- supervisor ingests a plant performance brief, evaluates which specialist domains have alert conditions, dispatches only the relevant subagents (not all nine each time), collects typed DomainReport objects from each, and consolidates into a unified PlantIntelligenceDashboard with cross-domain patterns and recommended priorities
queue.description: Plant intelligence supervisor: receive a daily plant brief, identify which operational domains have active issues, dispatch relevant specialist subagents conditionally, and synthesise their typed reports into an executive PlantIntelligenceDashboard with cross-domain root-cause patterns and recommended actions.
queue.synthetic_data: Plant performance brief with weekly summary metrics across all 9 domains. Include deliberate multi-domain alerts: high scrap on Line 3 + high downtime on Line 3 + capacity utilisation below target on Line 3 -- all pointing to one root cause that the supervisor should surface.

queue.harness_focus: BOM tree traversal tool + cost rollup Python calculator + variance flagging -- agent walks a multi-level bill of materials using a BOM lookup tool, accumulates material/labour/overhead costs at each node using a deterministic cost-rollup calculator, rolls up to finished goods level, and flags any node where actual cost deviates from standard by more than a configurable threshold
queue.description: Compute the full standard cost of a finished good by traversing its bill of materials. Agent walks the BOM tree, retrieves component costs from a price list tool, computes material+labour+overhead at each level using a Python cost-rollup tool, and produces a structured ProductCostCard with per-component cost drivers and variance flags.
queue.synthetic_data: BOM for 3-5 finished goods with 3-4 levels each (raw materials -> sub-assemblies -> finished goods). Component price list with standard costs. Current actual costs for 15-20% of components deviating >5% from standard. Include overhead rates by work centre.

queue.harness_focus: Pareto-ranked defect analysis + fishbone root-cause hypothesis generation -- agent aggregates scrap transactions by defect code/product/line/shift, calls a Python Pareto tool to identify causes of 80% of scrap cost, generates structured root-cause hypotheses using the 6M fishbone framework (Machine, Material, Method, Man, Measurement, Mother Nature) for the top-3 defect codes
queue.description: Analyse production scrap data to surface vital-few defect drivers and generate actionable root-cause hypotheses. Agent aggregates scrap by defect code, product, line, and shift; runs a Pareto ranking to identify causes of 80% of scrap cost; and generates structured 6M fishbone root-cause hypotheses for the top-3 codes.
queue.synthetic_data: Scrap transaction log for one month: 500-1000 records with (date, shift, line, product, defect_code, scrapped_qty, unit_cost). 8-12 defect codes with Pareto-distributed frequency (top 3 = 70% of scrap value). Include a shift-pattern anomaly: Code D003 spikes on C-shift exclusively.

queue.harness_focus: real EIA API integration + energy intensity benchmarking -- agent fetches current industrial electricity prices for the plant's US state from the EIA Open Data API (free key), computes plant energy intensity (kWh per unit produced) from meter readings, benchmarks against EIA industrial average for the sector, identifies the top-3 energy-intensive assets by consumption share, and estimates annual cost impact of a 10% intensity reduction
queue.description: Benchmark a manufacturing plant's energy performance against regional industrial averages. Agent reads plant energy meter data, fetches current electricity prices from the EIA API, computes energy intensity per unit produced, identifies high-consumption assets, and estimates the financial impact of achievable efficiency improvements.
queue.synthetic_data: Plant energy meter readings: monthly kWh by asset/work-centre for 12 months. Production volume by product for same period. Asset master: asset ID, description, rated power (kW), operating hours/month. 15-20 assets total. EIA API provides live regional price data.
queue.free_api: EIA Open Data API (api.eia.gov -- free API key from eia.gov, instant approval, no cost; series ELEC.PRICE.{state}-IND.M for monthly industrial electricity price by state)

queue.harness_focus: standard vs actual cost decomposition with efficiency/rate split (Python tool, not LLM arithmetic) -- agent receives production order actuals, calls a Python variance-decomposition tool that separates total variance into MPV (material price variance), MUV (material usage variance), LRV (labour rate variance), and LEV (labour efficiency variance), then interprets the decomposition in natural language with recommended corrective actions per driver
queue.description: Compute and interpret production cost variances for a batch of production orders. Agent compares actual vs standard quantities and rates for materials and labour, uses a Python decomposition tool to split total variance into MPV/MUV/LRV/LEV components, ranks by materiality, and drafts a concise management interpretation with corrective action recommendations.
queue.synthetic_data: 50-100 production orders with standard and actual: material quantities and prices per component, labour hours and rates per work centre. Standards from BOM and routing. Include systematic variances (all orders on Line-2 have +12% labour hours vs standard) and one-off events (spot material purchase at +20% price).

queue.harness_focus: SPC rule engine + Cpk/Ppk computation (Python tool) -- agent applies the 8 Western Electric SPC rules to measurement data, identifies out-of-control signals with rule citation, computes Cp/Cpk/Pp/Ppk process capability indices using a Python tool, and returns a structured SPCReport with out-of-control points, capability class (world-class/capable/marginal/incapable), and corrective action triggers
queue.description: Assess production process capability and identify out-of-control signals. Agent applies Western Electric SPC rules to quality measurement data, computes process capability indices (Cpk, Ppk), flags out-of-control signals with specific rule violated, and generates a structured SPC report with corrective actions based on the capability classification.
queue.synthetic_data: Measurement data for 3-5 critical quality characteristics: 200-500 measurements per characteristic with USL, LSL, and target. Inject rule violations: Rule 1 (1 point >3-sigma), Rule 2 (9 consecutive points same side of mean), Rule 5 (2 of 3 consecutive >2-sigma). One characteristic with Cpk <1.0 to flag incapable process.

queue.harness_focus: OEE decomposition (Availability x Performance x Quality) + bottleneck identification -- agent computes OEE and its three factors at machine/cell/line/plant level using a Python OEE calculator tool, identifies which loss category dominates at each level, and ranks assets by OEE-weighted impact on total plant throughput to identify the binding bottleneck
queue.description: Compute Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) across a manufacturing plant. Agent calculates OEE and its three component factors (availability, performance rate, quality rate) at machine, cell, and line level; identifies the dominant loss category per asset; ranks assets by throughput impact; and produces a CapacityUtilizationReport with bottleneck identification.
queue.synthetic_data: Machine log for 10-20 machines over 4 weeks: scheduled time, unplanned downtime (minutes), planned downtime (minutes), ideal cycle time (units/hour), actual output, quality rejects. Design a clear bottleneck: one machine with OEE <50% on the critical path, others at 70-85%.

queue.harness_focus: Pareto loss analysis + MTBF/MTTR computation (Python tool) + maintenance priority matrix -- agent aggregates downtime events by failure code and asset, calls a Python tool to compute MTBF and MTTR per asset, runs Pareto ranking on failure codes by total lost production hours, and outputs a MaintenancePriorityMatrix ranking assets by risk score = (1/MTBF) x MTTR x production_cost_per_hour
queue.description: Analyse manufacturing downtime events to produce a maintenance priority matrix. Agent aggregates downtime by failure code and asset, computes MTBF and MTTR using a Python calculator, ranks failure codes by total production loss (Pareto), and outputs a MaintenancePriorityMatrix prioritising assets where high failure frequency combines with long repair times.
queue.synthetic_data: Downtime event log for 10-15 assets over 6 months: 300-500 events with (asset_id, failure_code, start_datetime, end_datetime, repair_action, technician). 8-12 failure codes Pareto-distributed. Include one asset with low MTBF + high MTTR (chronic problem) and one with high MTBF + low MTTR (nuisance but manageable).

queue.harness_focus: threshold-gated KPI alerting + trend classification + exception narrative -- agent evaluates each plant KPI against configured red/amber/green thresholds, classifies each metric as improving/stable/degrading using a rolling 4-period trend rule (3 consecutive periods in same direction = confirmed trend), drafts a one-page plant performance summary with exception narrative and recommended focus areas
queue.description: Monitor weekly plant performance KPIs against targets and generate an exception-driven management summary. Agent checks each KPI (OEE, yield, scrap%, OTIF, energy intensity, cost per unit) against RAG thresholds, classifies trend direction, flags any KPI crossing from amber to red, and drafts a structured one-page performance summary for the plant manager.
queue.synthetic_data: 13 weeks of weekly KPI data for 6-8 plant metrics per line and plant. Design: OEE trending down 3 weeks (crosses amber at week 11), scrap% spiking at week 12, OTIF stable but below target throughout. Include one metric recovering (energy intensity improving for 4 consecutive weeks).

queue.harness_focus: remaining useful life estimation from proxy indicators + cost-of-failure vs scheduled-maintenance NPV tradeoff -- agent estimates RUL from asset age, maintenance history, and recent OEE trend decline using configurable heuristic degradation curves (no ML -- deterministic Python); computes cost-of-failure scenario (repair cost + lost production) vs scheduled maintenance scenario; outputs a MaintenanceDecisionMatrix with NPV breakeven point per asset
queue.description: Build a predictive maintenance cost model for critical plant assets. Agent estimates remaining useful life using age-based degradation curves and recent performance trend signals, models cost-of-failure vs cost-of-scheduled-maintenance, computes NPV breakeven for maintenance timing, and outputs a structured MaintenanceDecisionMatrix.
queue.synthetic_data: Asset master for 8-12 critical assets: acquisition date, design life (years), last planned maintenance date, maintenance cost, replacement cost. OEE trend data last 12 weeks (declining for 2-3 assets). Downtime cost estimate per hour by asset class. Include one asset past design life with no recent maintenance.

queue.harness_focus: coordinated inventory health orchestration + risk register synthesis ranked by financial exposure -- supervisor dispatches all six inventory subagents, collects typed InventoryHealthReport objects from each, de-duplicates cross-agent findings on the same SKU, computes total financial exposure per risk category (excess stock, obsolete stock, stockout risk, material variance), and synthesises a unified InventoryRiskRegister prioritised by dollar exposure
queue.description: Orchestrate a complete inventory health review. Supervisor dispatches aging, optimisation, SLOB, spare parts, material consumption, and reorder subagents; aggregates typed reports; ranks findings by financial exposure; and produces an executive InventoryRiskRegister with prioritised corrective actions and estimated P&L impact.
queue.synthetic_data: Combined inputs from all six subagents sharing one SKU master, movement history, BOM, and supplier data. Design cross-agent findings: SLOB agent and aging agent both flag the same high-value SKU for different reasons; reorder agent and optimisation agent propose conflicting order quantities for another SKU.

queue.harness_focus: aging bucket classification + carrying cost computation + IAS-2-aligned provision recommendation -- agent segments inventory into age buckets (<30/30-90/90-180/>180 days since last movement), calls a Python carrying-cost tool (holding rate x value x days), applies an IAS 2 net-realisable-value test to estimate required write-down provision per bucket, and recommends disposition action (retain/mark-down/return-to-supplier/scrap) per bucket
queue.description: Classify inventory by age since last movement, compute carrying costs per aging bucket, apply an IAS 2 NRV assessment to estimate required write-down provisions, and produce a structured InventoryAgingReport with disposition recommendations per bucket.
queue.synthetic_data: Stock ledger for 500-1000 SKUs: SKU code, description, category, quantity on hand, unit cost, last movement date, warehouse location. Distribution: 60% fresh (<30 days), 20% 30-90 days, 12% 90-180 days, 8% >180 days. Include high-value items (>$5k unit cost) in aged buckets for material write-down exposure.

queue.harness_focus: EOQ + safety stock + reorder point computation (Python tool) + policy gap analysis -- agent computes Economic Order Quantity, safety stock (z-score x sigma x sqrt(lead_time)), and reorder point per SKU using a Python inventory-policy tool; compares computed targets against current parameters; flags SKUs where current policy creates excess holding cost (over-stocked by >25%) or unacceptable stockout risk (safety stock < 1 week demand)
queue.description: Compute optimal inventory policies (EOQ, safety stock, reorder point) per SKU using demand history and supply parameters. Agent runs Python-based inventory optimisation formulas, compares computed targets against current stock parameters, and flags SKUs where current policy is causing material excess holding cost or stockout risk.
queue.synthetic_data: SKU demand history: 52 weeks of weekly demand per SKU (100-200 SKUs) modelled with Poisson distribution (mean 10-200 units/week). Supplier lead times (1-8 weeks). Holding cost rate (25% per annum). Ordering cost per PO ($50-$200). Current reorder points and order quantities for comparison against computed optima.

queue.harness_focus: velocity quartile classification + disposal channel decision matrix -- agent classifies SKUs into velocity quartiles (fast/medium/slow/no-move) using a Python quartile tool, applies SLOB criteria (slow-moving = bottom quartile >90 days; obsolete = no movement >180 days + no open sales orders), and recommends disposal channel per SKU using a decision matrix (liquidate/discount-sale/return-to-supplier/repurpose/scrap/write-off) based on age, value, condition, and return-policy eligibility
queue.description: Identify slow-moving and obsolete (SLOB) inventory and recommend disposal channels. Agent classifies SKUs by movement velocity, applies SLOB criteria, matches each SLOB candidate against a disposal decision matrix, and outputs a structured SLOBRegister with recommended action and estimated recovery value per SKU.
queue.synthetic_data: Movement history for 500-2000 SKUs: monthly units sold/consumed for 12 months. Inject 10-15% slow-moving (last sale >90 days) and 5-8% no-movement (no sale in 180+ days). Include high-value SLOB items (>$1k unit cost, >100 units on hand) for financial materiality.

queue.harness_focus: criticality-weighted stocking policy + stockout cost vs holding cost tradeoff -- agent scores each spare part by equipment criticality class (A=safety-critical, B=production-critical, C=non-critical) and supplier lead time, computes minimum stocking level using a service-level target per criticality class, estimates cost-of-stockout (hourly downtime cost x MTTR) vs annual holding cost per part, flags parts where stockout risk exceeds holding cost by >10x
queue.description: Rationalise spare parts stocking policy. Agent scores parts by criticality and lead time, computes recommended min/max stocking levels using criticality-adjusted service level targets, estimates cost-of-stockout against annual holding cost, and flags dangerously understocked parts (high criticality + long lead time + zero stock).
queue.synthetic_data: Spare parts catalogue for a manufacturing plant: 200-400 parts with (part_number, description, equipment_id, criticality_class, supplier_lead_time_weeks, current_qty, unit_cost, annual_usage, last_usage_date). Include critical parts with zero stock and 6+ week lead times to generate clear stocking recommendations.

queue.harness_focus: BOM-vs-actual material consumption comparison + deviation classification -- agent compares actual material issues to BOM-expected quantities per production order using a Python yield-ratio calculator, flags orders with >10% deviation, and classifies likely root causes (yield loss from scrap/rework, over-issue to buffer, BOM master-data error, shrinkage)
queue.description: Monitor material consumption against BOM expectations per production order. Agent computes actual-vs-BOM yield ratios per component, flags production orders with abnormal consumption, classifies the likely cause of each deviation, and outputs a structured MaterialConsumptionAlert with investigation recommendations.
queue.synthetic_data: Production order BOM issue records: 100-200 orders with actual material issued vs BOM-required quantity per component. Inject anomaly patterns: systematic over-issue on component COMP-007 (BOM error candidate), random spikes on COMP-012 (scrap events), high deviation on all components for ORDER-089 (rework order).

queue.harness_focus: multi-constraint purchase requisition generation -- agent identifies items below reorder point (Python stock-check tool), generates a PR for each, rounds up to supplier MOQ, deduplicates against open POs (no-double-ordering tool), validates aggregate PR value against period budget cap, and outputs a prioritised ReorderPlan sorted by days-of-stock-remaining
queue.description: Generate a prioritised reorder plan for items below reorder point. Agent checks current stock against ROP, generates PRs respecting supplier MOQ, filters items already covered by open POs, validates total PR value stays within period budget, and outputs a ReorderPlan ranked by urgency (days of stock remaining) with supplier and cost details.
queue.synthetic_data: Current stock levels for 200-500 SKUs (qty on hand, reorder point, safety stock). Open PO register (qty on order, expected receipt date). Supplier catalogue (MOQ, lead time, unit price). Period procurement budget cap. Inject: 15-25% of SKUs below ROP, some already covered by open POs, a few where MOQ forces a large order.

queue.harness_focus: spend analytics orchestration + strategic sourcing brief synthesis with cross-agent deduplication -- supervisor ingests a spend extract and ASL, dispatches all six procurement subagents, aggregates typed findings by category and supplier, de-duplicates cross-agent insights on the same supplier or commodity, and synthesises an executive StrategicSourcingBrief with prioritised savings opportunities and risk mitigations ranked by financial impact
queue.description: Orchestrate a full procurement intelligence review. Supervisor dispatches specialist agents for spend analysis, supplier performance, price variance, contract compliance, vendor risk, and sourcing strategy; de-duplicates cross-agent findings; and produces a StrategicSourcingBrief for the CPO with priority actions ranked by financial impact.
queue.synthetic_data: Shared spend extract, supplier performance records, contract extracts, and approved supplier list. Design cross-agent findings: same supplier flagged independently by performance (OTIF issues) and risk (country risk) and contract compliance (off-contract spend) agents -- supervisor must surface the convergence.

queue.harness_focus: UNSPSC taxonomy classification + addressable spend cube + savings opportunity quantification -- agent classifies spend lines into 2-digit UNSPSC commodity segments using an LLM classifier tool, aggregates spend into a cube (segment x supplier x cost-centre x period), identifies addressable spend pools (consolidated-buyable vs maverick vs spot), and estimates savings potential per addressable segment using configurable benchmark discount rates
queue.description: Build a spend intelligence analysis from raw purchase data. Agent classifies spend lines by UNSPSC commodity, constructs a spend cube, identifies addressable spend by category, estimates savings potential using benchmark rates, and flags high-maverick-spend categories where contract consolidation yields quick wins.
queue.synthetic_data: 500-2000 purchase order lines: supplier name, invoice description, amount, cost centre, period. Include 15-20% maverick (off-contract) spend across 3-4 categories. Design one category with 3+ suppliers where consolidation opportunity is clear. Apply 80/20 distribution (top 20% suppliers = 80% of spend).

queue.harness_focus: multi-KPI weighted scorecard + spend-vs-performance segmentation matrix -- agent aggregates OTIF, quality reject rate, invoice accuracy, and responsiveness KPIs per supplier using configurable weights, computes a composite performance score, positions suppliers on a 2x2 spend-vs-performance matrix, segments into strategic/preferred/qualified/at-risk tiers, and generates structured SupplierActionCards for at-risk suppliers
queue.description: Score and segment suppliers by performance. Agent aggregates delivery, quality, and commercial KPIs per supplier, computes weighted composite scores, positions suppliers on a spend-vs-performance matrix, segments into tiers, and generates structured action cards for at-risk suppliers with recommended development plan or exit timeline.
queue.synthetic_data: Supplier delivery records for 20-50 suppliers: PO#, planned delivery date, actual delivery date, ordered qty, delivered qty, quality reject qty, invoice accuracy flag. 12 months of data. Include 3-5 suppliers with OTIF below 85%, 2-3 with quality reject rates >3%.

queue.harness_focus: PPV decomposition into contract/market/spec drivers + BLS PPI benchmarking for market-price validation -- agent computes purchase price variance per PO line (actual vs standard), classifies each variance into contract-drift/market-price-move/spec-change using a driver-classification tool, optionally fetches BLS PPI series for the commodity to validate market-price driver claims, and recommends buyer action per systematic variance type
queue.description: Analyse purchase price variance by supplier and commodity. Agent computes PPV per PO line, decomposes variances into contract, market-price, and specification-change drivers, validates market-price driver claims against BLS PPI data for the commodity, distinguishes systematic from one-off variances, and outputs a PPVReport with buyer action plan.
queue.synthetic_data: 300-500 PO lines with standard cost and actual invoice price across 5-8 commodity categories and 15-25 suppliers over 6 months. Inject: systematic contract-drift for one supplier (+8% above standard consistently), market-driven spike for a commodity (justified by PPI movement), spec-change event on one product.
queue.free_api: Bureau of Labor Statistics PPI API (api.bls.gov -- no key for basic queries; WPS series for commodity producer price indices; use to validate market-price PPV driver claims)

queue.harness_focus: obligation extraction from contract text + actual-vs-committed gap analysis with value-leakage quantification -- agent reads supplier contract clauses, extracts structured ContractObligations (pricing tiers, volume rebate thresholds, SLA obligations, payment terms), compares each obligation against actual spend and delivery data, flags compliance gaps, and quantifies value leakage per gap (e.g. unclaimed rebate = 7% x $480k = $33.6k missed)
queue.description: Audit supplier contracts against actual purchasing behaviour. Agent extracts commercial obligations from contract excerpts, compares each against actual spend/delivery data, flags compliance gaps (missing rebate claims, SLA breach patterns, off-contract spend in exclusivity categories), and outputs a ContractComplianceReport with estimated value leakage per gap.
queue.synthetic_data: 3-5 supplier contract excerpts with pricing tiers, volume rebate structures (5% at $500k, 7% at $1M), SLA commitments, exclusivity clauses. Corresponding actual spend and delivery records. Design deliberate gaps: $480k spend when $500k rebate trigger was achievable, SLA breach pattern over 3 months.

queue.harness_focus: multi-dimension vendor risk scoring with two real public API calls -- agent scores vendors across: (1) financial health (synthetic financials: revenue trend, debt ratio, interest coverage); (2) geopolitical risk (World Bank Governance Indicators API -- same pattern as example 30); (3) regulatory standing (USASpending.gov API -- fetches federal contract history and checks for debarment flags); combines into a weighted VendorRiskScore and outputs a structured VendorRiskCard per supplier
queue.description: Produce a multi-dimension vendor risk assessment. Agent evaluates financial health from company financials, fetches geopolitical risk from the World Bank API, checks federal contract history and debarment via USASpending.gov, combines into a weighted composite risk score, and outputs a VendorRiskCard per supplier with recommended due diligence actions.
queue.synthetic_data: Vendor profiles for 10-15 suppliers: company name, country, 3 years of revenue and total debt, EBIT, payment terms. Include vendors from 3-4 countries with varying World Bank governance scores. USASpending and World Bank APIs provide live data.
queue.free_api: USASpending.gov API (api.usaspending.gov -- no key required; POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award/ for vendor contract history); World Bank Governance Indicators API (same pattern as example 30 -- no key required)

queue.harness_focus: ASL-constrained sourcing strategy recommendation with compliance gate -- agent matches commodity requirements against an approved supplier list (ASL lookup tool), checks compliance constraints (single-source risk threshold, geographic concentration limit, annual spend cap per supplier), evaluates each compliant supplier against capability criteria, and recommends sourcing strategy (single/dual/multi-source/open tender) per commodity with rationale and risk flags
queue.description: Recommend a sourcing strategy for each commodity category using the approved supplier list as the primary constraint. Agent matches requirements against ASL-qualified suppliers, checks compliance constraints, evaluates capability fit, recommends a sourcing strategy, and flags categories where ASL coverage is insufficient (triggering a new supplier development recommendation).
queue.synthetic_data: Commodity requirements list (10-15 commodities with volume, quality spec, lead time requirement, geographic constraint). Approved supplier list with capabilities, certifications, geographic location, spend history. Include 2-3 commodities with single-source risk and 1-2 with no qualified ASL supplier.

queue.harness_focus: end-to-end logistics performance orchestration + cross-domain pattern detection -- supervisor dispatches all five logistics subagents, receives typed LogisticsReport objects, identifies cross-domain patterns spanning cost/delivery/fleet/fuel dimensions (e.g. same carrier = high cost + poor OTIF + poor fuel efficiency = carrier capability issue), and synthesises a FreightIntelligenceBrief with cross-cutting root cause hypotheses
queue.description: Orchestrate a weekly logistics performance review. Supervisor dispatches cost, scheduling, delivery, fleet, and fuel subagents, identifies patterns spanning multiple domains, and produces a structured FreightIntelligenceBrief for the Head of Logistics with prioritised actions and estimated cost impact.
queue.synthetic_data: Shared shipment records, fleet telematics, and fuel card data. Design cross-domain patterns: Carrier B appears in cost-overrun report + worst-OTIF report + poor-fuel-efficiency report -- all pointing to same carrier capability issue for supervisor to surface.

queue.harness_focus: lane-level freight cost benchmarking + carrier mix optimisation -- agent computes actual cost-per-kg and cost-per-lane for each origin-destination-mode combination, benchmarks against contracted rate card targets using a rate lookup tool, identifies lanes where actual spend exceeds contract rate by >15%, and proposes carrier mix adjustments with estimated annual savings
queue.description: Benchmark freight spend against contracted rate cards at lane level and identify carrier mix optimisation opportunities. Agent computes actual vs contracted cost per lane/mode/weight break, flags lanes with material overspend, and outputs a LogisticsCostOptimizationPlan with estimated annual savings per recommendation.
queue.synthetic_data: Shipment records for 3 months: 200-500 shipments with (origin_zip, destination_zip, mode, weight_kg, carrier, actual_cost, contracted_rate). 3-5 carriers per lane. Include lanes where one carrier is consistently used despite another having a lower contracted rate.

queue.harness_focus: constraint-satisfied load-to-vehicle assignment + real OSRM routing API -- agent allocates delivery orders to available vehicles respecting weight capacity, volume capacity, time-window constraints, and delivery priority using a greedy assignment tool; calls the OSRM public API for real-world routing distance and duration between consecutive stops; outputs a daily TransportPlan with sequenced routes, ETA per stop, and vehicle utilisation
queue.description: Build a daily transport schedule allocating delivery orders to vehicles. Agent assigns loads to vehicles respecting capacity and time windows, sequences stops to minimise route distance using OSRM routing calls for real distance/duration, computes ETA per stop, flags unassigned orders, and outputs a structured TransportPlan.
queue.synthetic_data: Daily delivery order set: 20-40 orders with (customer, delivery_address_lat_lng, weight_kg, volume_m3, delivery_time_window, priority). Fleet: 5-8 vehicles with (capacity_kg, capacity_m3, home_depot_lat_lng). Use real US city lat/lng coordinates for OSRM to return realistic routing data.
queue.free_api: OSRM public demo API (router.project-osrm.org/route/v1/driving/{lon1},{lat1};{lon2},{lat2} -- no key required; rate-limit to 1 req/s in demos; returns real routing distance_km and duration_s between coordinate pairs)

queue.harness_focus: OTIF scoring + multi-dimension failure mode Pareto -- agent computes on-time delivery rate and in-full delivery rate per carrier/lane/customer/period using a Python metrics calculator tool, classifies late and short-delivery root causes by reason code (carrier delay/customs hold/traffic/warehouse error/weather), runs Pareto ranking on failure modes by frequency and cost impact, and outputs a DeliveryPerformanceReport with trend data and failure mode ranking
queue.description: Compute and diagnose delivery performance (OTIF) across carriers, lanes, and customers. Agent calculates on-time and in-full rates per dimension, classifies delivery failures by root cause, runs a Pareto analysis on failure modes, identifies vital-few causes driving 80% of OTIF failures, and outputs a structured DeliveryPerformanceReport.
queue.synthetic_data: Delivery records for 3 months: 500-1000 shipments with (shipment_id, carrier, origin, destination, customer, planned_delivery_date, actual_delivery_date, ordered_qty, delivered_qty, reason_code_if_late). Include: 85% OTIF baseline with deliberate failure patterns (one carrier 70% OTIF on long-haul, one lane with seasonal weather delays).

queue.harness_focus: asset utilisation + empty-run rate analysis + backhaul opportunity matching -- agent computes loaded-km / total-km utilisation per vehicle, identifies vehicles with empty-run rate >30%, cross-references empty return legs with available shipment demand on the same corridors, and recommends specific backhaul opportunities or fleet rebalancing moves with estimated cost saving per recommendation
queue.description: Analyse fleet utilisation and identify empty-run reduction opportunities. Agent computes utilisation rates per vehicle, flags vehicles with high empty-run percentages, matches empty return legs against available shipment demand on the same corridors, recommends backhaul opportunities, and outputs a FleetUtilizationReport with rebalancing recommendations.
queue.synthetic_data: Vehicle trip log for one month: 8-12 vehicles, 200-400 trips with (vehicle_id, trip_date, origin, destination, load_status: loaded/empty, distance_km, weight_carried). Fleet master: vehicle capacity, home depot. Daily unassigned shipment demand on key corridors as backhaul opportunity pool.

queue.harness_focus: real EIA diesel price API + cost-per-km benchmarking + outlier detection -- agent fetches current regional diesel prices from the EIA Open Data API (free key), computes actual fuel cost per km per vehicle using fuel card and trip records, benchmarks each vehicle/route against a fleet-wide fuel efficiency target (litres/100km), flags vehicles >15% above fleet average using a Python outlier tool, and estimates annual saving from bringing outliers to fleet mean
queue.description: Monitor fleet fuel efficiency and estimate the financial impact of outlier vehicles. Agent reads fuel card and trip records, fetches current regional diesel prices from the EIA API, computes fuel cost per km per vehicle, benchmarks against fleet target, identifies fuel-inefficient vehicles and routes, and outputs a FuelConsumptionReport.
queue.synthetic_data: Fuel card records for 8-12 vehicles over 3 months: (vehicle_id, date, litres_filled, cost_local_currency, location). Matched trip records: (vehicle_id, trip_date, km_driven, load_weight_kg). Include 2-3 vehicles with fuel consumption 20-30% above fleet average (maintenance issue candidates).
queue.free_api: EIA Open Data API (api.eia.gov -- free key from eia.gov; series PET.EMD_EPD2D_PTE_{state}_DPG.W for weekly diesel retail prices by US state, or PET.EMD_EPD2D_PTE_NUS_DPG.W for US national average)

queue.harness_focus: cross-domain strategic briefing synthesis + executive materiality filter -- supervisor aggregates typed KPIAlert and ESGStatus objects from subagents, applies an executive materiality filter (only items exceeding significance threshold bubble up), optionally integrates domain supervisor reports (manufacturing/inventory/procurement/logistics) as signal inputs, and produces a weekly C-suite ExecutiveBrief with exception narrative and forward-looking risk flags
queue.description: Produce a weekly executive intelligence brief for the C-suite. Supervisor receives KPI and ESG subagent reports, applies materiality filtering, integrates optional domain-specific alerts from manufacturing/logistics/procurement supervisors, and synthesises a structured ExecutiveBrief: top-3 business exceptions requiring CEO attention, forward-looking risk flags, and recommended executive actions.
queue.synthetic_data: Aggregated KPI and ESG data from subagent outputs plus brief excerpts from domain supervisor reports. Design: one business KPI below threshold + one ESG metric missing a disclosure deadline + one manufacturing alert -- all requiring coordinated executive response that the supervisor should identify.

queue.harness_focus: threshold-gated multi-dimensional KPI alerting + 4-period trend classification -- agent evaluates business KPIs across four dimensions (financial: revenue/EBITDA/cash; operational: OTIF/OEE/yield; customer: NPS/churn/CSAT; people: headcount/attrition/engagement) against red/amber/green thresholds, classifies each metric trend as improving/stable/deteriorating using a rolling 4-period rule, flags any metric crossing into red, and drafts an executive exception summary with recommended escalation actions
queue.description: Monitor a comprehensive business KPI dashboard and generate an exception-driven executive summary. Agent evaluates KPIs across financial, operational, customer, and people dimensions against configured RAG thresholds, applies trend classification, identifies material exceptions, and drafts a structured C-suite KPI Alert with exception narrative and recommended actions.
queue.synthetic_data: 13 weeks of weekly KPI data across 12-16 metrics in four dimensions. Design exception patterns: revenue trending below forecast for 4 weeks (amber to red), NPS declining for 3 weeks, OEE stable but below target. Include one metric recovering (cash position improving after 8 weeks of concern) to show balanced reporting.

queue.harness_focus: GHG Protocol scope 1/2/3 calculation (Python tool with embedded IPCC AR6 emission factors) + multi-framework disclosure structuring -- agent computes Scope 1 (direct combustion: fuel_quantity x fuel-specific IPCC emission factor), Scope 2 market-based (energy consumption x supplier emission factor) and location-based (energy x EPA eGRID regional factor), and material Scope 3 categories (business travel, upstream spend-based); structures output against both SEC climate disclosure requirements and CSRD Article 29a categories; flags data gaps requiring supplemental disclosure
queue.description: Compute GHG emissions and structure a climate disclosure. Agent ingests activity data (fuel by type, electricity by site, business travel miles, supply chain spend), applies IPCC AR6 emission factors using a Python calculator tool, computes Scope 1/2/3 emissions with market-based and location-based Scope 2 variants, structures output against SEC climate rules and CSRD categories, and flags data gaps.
queue.synthetic_data: Annual activity data: fuel consumption by fuel type (natural gas m3, diesel litres, LPG kg) per facility; electricity consumption kWh by site with supplier emission factor; business travel miles by mode (air/rail/car); supply chain spend by EEIO category. Include one site with no electricity emission factor to trigger a gap flag. IPCC AR6 emission factors are public domain -- embed as constants in tools.py.
queue.free_api: EPA eGRID regional emission factors (epa.gov/egrid -- downloadable table, no API; use as lookup constants for Scope 2 location-based calculations); IPCC AR6 WG3 Annex III emission factors (public domain -- embed directly in tools.py as a dict)

queue.harness_focus: interrupt-and-resume human approval gate -- agent proposes an irreversible action (post a journal entry, revoke access, send a collections notice) as a structured ProposedAction object, execution pauses via a checkpointed interrupt, a human approves/edits/rejects through a typed ApprovalDecision, and only an approved action reaches the tool-call boundary; rejected or edited actions are logged with the human's rationale and never silently retried -- the lesson is that 'route to an approval tier' (as labelled in ids 45 and 47) is not the same as a harness that actually gates execution on a human decision
queue.description: Minimal LangGraph example demonstrating interrupt()/Command(resume=...): the agent drafts a journal entry, an access revocation, or a collections letter as a typed ProposedAction, the graph halts at a human-review node, a simulated approver responds approve/edit/reject, and only 'approve' resumes execution to the node that actually fires the tool. Becomes a reusable prerequisite for any example with an irreversible action.
queue.synthetic_data: 3 scenarios exercising all 3 resume paths: a clean approve, an edit-then-approve (human changes the dollar amount before resuming), and a reject (human blocks the action and supplies a rationale that gets logged, not discarded).

queue.harness_focus: hierarchical security signal triage + conditional subagent dispatch + materiality-gated escalation -- supervisor ingests a daily security signal feed (vuln scan deltas, auth anomalies, dependency advisories, access-review diffs), dispatches only the subagents whose domain shows an active signal, collects typed SecurityFindingReport objects, and synthesises a SecurityPostureBrief; any P0/P1 finding is routed through the approval-gate pattern (id 90) before a recommended action is treated as actionable rather than merely advisory
queue.description: Daily security operations brief. Supervisor receives a signal digest, conditionally dispatches threat-triage, access-governance, vulnerability-remediation, incident-response, and compliance-evidence subagents, synthesises a board-ready SecurityPostureBrief, and gates any high-severity recommended action behind the human approval pattern from id 90 -- the same conditional-dispatch-and-synthesise shape as id 57, applied to security instead of plant operations.
queue.synthetic_data: Daily signal digest combining 15-20 SIEM-style auth events (5 anomalous: impossible-travel, off-hours admin login), a dependency-scan delta (3 new CVEs, 1 critical), an IAM diff (2 new admin grants, 1 orphaned service account), and an open incident ticket. Design one cross-domain correlation: the orphaned service account is the same identity behind the off-hours login -- the supervisor should surface this the way id 57 surfaces the Line-3 multi-domain pattern.

queue.harness_focus: MITRE ATT&CK tactic classification + composite severity scoring + false-positive dedup against a known-pattern allowlist -- agent classifies each raw security alert into a MITRE ATT&CK tactic/technique, scores composite severity from signal strength x asset criticality, and suppresses alerts matching a configured false-positive allowlist instead of escalating known-benign noise
queue.description: Classify and triage raw security alerts. Agent maps each alert to a MITRE ATT&CK tactic, computes a composite severity score, deduplicates against known false-positive patterns, and returns a ranked typed TriageReport so the supervisor only sees signal, not noise.
queue.synthetic_data: 40-60 raw alert records (auth, network, endpoint) spanning 8-10 MITRE tactics. Include 30% known-benign patterns (scheduled backup job flagged as 'unusual process', corporate VPN range flagged as 'impossible travel') that the allowlist should suppress.

queue.harness_focus: privilege-escalation diff detection + segregation-of-duties matrix cross-reference + mandatory human approval before any revoke recommendation is marked executable -- agent compares current IAM role assignments against the prior snapshot, flags new admin/privileged grants and orphaned accounts (no login in N days but active privileges), cross-references grant pairs against a configured SoD matrix, and emits any 'revoke' recommendation as a ProposedAction routed through the approval-gate pattern (id 90) rather than a free-text suggestion
queue.description: Detect privilege-escalation and segregation-of-duties violations from an IAM snapshot diff. Agent flags new privileged grants, orphaned active-privilege accounts, and SoD conflicts (e.g. one identity holding both vendor-create and vendor-approve), and emits any revoke recommendation as a gated ProposedAction rather than a direct action.
queue.synthetic_data: Two IAM snapshots (30 identities, 8 role types) one week apart. Inject: 2 new admin grants with no change-ticket reference, 1 orphaned account (90+ days inactive, still holds prod-write), 1 SoD conflict (same identity in both AP-create and AP-approve roles).

queue.harness_focus: constraint-satisfied patch sequencing -- agent extends the OSV.dev CVE lookup pattern (id 28) with a Python scheduling tool that sequences remediation across a maintenance-window constraint (one change-freeze window per system, max N concurrent patches per environment) and dependency ordering (patch the library before the service that pins it), producing a typed RemediationPlan rather than a flat severity-sorted list
queue.description: Turn a ranked vulnerability list into an executable patch plan. Agent reuses the OSV.dev lookup pattern from example 28, then calls a Python constraint-scheduling tool to sequence patches across maintenance windows and dependency order, returning a typed RemediationPlan with a target date per system.
queue.synthetic_data: 12-15 vulnerable packages across 4 systems with declared maintenance windows (weekly, 4-hour) and a small dependency graph (2 packages must patch before a 3rd). Include one CVE the OSV.dev API returns as actively-exploited that should jump the queue regardless of window.
queue.free_api: OSV.dev (api.osv.dev -- no key, same pattern as example 28)

queue.harness_focus: structured incident runbook generation + stakeholder-tiered communication drafting + do-not-auto-send gate -- agent classifies a security incident's blast radius and severity, selects the matching runbook template, drafts tiered stakeholder comms (internal eng, leadership, customer-facing where applicable), and every comms draft is explicitly flagged not-for-auto-send, requiring the approval-gate pattern (id 90) before any message leaves the draft state
queue.description: Draft an incident response runbook and tiered stakeholder communications from a classified security incident. Every leadership- or customer-facing draft is held behind the approval-gate pattern rather than treated as ready-to-send.
queue.synthetic_data: 3 incident scenarios at increasing severity (contained internal misconfiguration, customer-data-adjacent exposure, active ransomware indicator) to exercise 3 different runbook templates and communication tiers.

queue.harness_focus: control-to-framework mapping + evidence-gap detection -- agent maps current security control state (from the other subagents' typed reports) against SOC2/ISO27001 control IDs, flags controls with stale or missing evidence, and produces a structured EvidenceGapReport citing the specific control ID and the missing artifact -- the citation-grounded-extraction lesson from ids 9 and 15, applied to internal controls instead of external documents
queue.description: Map current security posture to a compliance framework and surface evidence gaps. Agent consumes the typed reports from the threat-triage, access-governance, and vulnerability-remediation subagents, maps findings to SOC2/ISO27001 control IDs, and flags any control lacking current evidence with a citation to what is missing.
queue.synthetic_data: A control catalogue of 15-20 SOC2 controls mapped to evidence sources. Inject 4 controls with stale evidence (>90 days old) and 2 controls with no evidence on file at all.

queue.harness_focus: period-close controls orchestration + cross-subagent exception consolidation + mandatory human sign-off gate -- supervisor runs the close-cycle DAG by composing the already-built JV posting (id 44), bank reconciliation (id 46), fixed-asset (id 50), and fraud/SAR (id 55) agents as subagents, consolidates every subagent's typed exception output into a single ControlsExceptionRegister ranked by financial exposure, and routes any exception above a materiality threshold through the approval-gate pattern (id 90) to a human controller before the close is marked complete -- an org of individually-correct agents is not itself a control unless something enforces that a human signs off on the aggregate picture
queue.description: Orchestrate a period-close controls cycle across existing finance-accounting agents. Supervisor dispatches journal-entry validation, bank reconciliation, fixed-asset rollforward, and fraud/SAR screening as subagents (reused, not rebuilt), plus new segregation-of-duties and audit-trail subagents, consolidates every exception into a single ranked ControlsExceptionRegister, and requires human controller sign-off via the approval-gate pattern before the close can be marked complete. This is the concrete 'rectifying ledgers' org -- elevating four already-shipped standalone agents into a governed pipeline instead of leaving them as disconnected point tools.
queue.synthetic_data: One simulated close cycle assembled by composing the existing synthetic generators from ids 44/46/50 plus the new 15-role SoD matrix from id 98. Design one cross-subagent pattern deliberately: the JV agent flags an imbalanced manual entry posted by the same identity the SoD checker flags as a conflict, and the bank-rec agent's potential-fraud bucket references the same vendor -- the supervisor should connect these three signals into one elevated-risk exception instead of reporting them separately.

queue.harness_focus: SoD matrix cross-reference + conflict severity scoring -- agent cross-references who-prepared vs who-approved on every journal entry, payment run, and access grant against a configured segregation-of-duties matrix, scores each conflict by financial exposure (transaction amount x control criticality), and flags any identity holding both sides of a toxic-combination pair -- the classic internal-audit pattern, not previously represented anywhere in the queue
queue.description: Detect segregation-of-duties violations across the close cycle. Agent cross-references preparer/approver identities for journal entries, payments, and access grants against a configured SoD matrix, scores conflicts by financial exposure, and flags toxic-combination role holders.
queue.synthetic_data: Transaction log of 200-300 journal entries and payments with preparer/approver identity fields, plus a 15-role SoD matrix defining toxic combinations (e.g. AP-create + AP-approve, vendor-master-edit + payment-release). Inject 4-6 deliberate conflicts of varying financial exposure.

queue.harness_focus: citation-grounded audit narrative generation -- agent assembles a defensible 'what changed, who approved it, and why' narrative per close-cycle exception by citing the specific subagent finding, the approval-gate decision record, and the relevant control ID, refusing to assert any remediation occurred without a citable approval record -- the citation-grounded-extraction lesson from ids 9 and 15, applied to internally generated control evidence instead of external documents
queue.description: Generate a defensible audit narrative for each control exception raised during the close cycle. Agent cites the originating subagent finding, the human approval-gate decision and rationale, and the relevant control ID for every claim, and explicitly flags any exception that lacks a citable resolution record.
queue.synthetic_data: 5-8 close-cycle exceptions of varying type (JV imbalance, bank-rec fraud flag, SoD conflict), a mix of fully-resolved (has an approval record) and unresolved (no sign-off yet) status, to exercise both the cited-resolution path and the flagged-gap path.

queue.harness_focus: framework-comparison sibling of id 1 -- identical tool-calling task (same tool set and prompt contract as the basic ReAct agent) reimplemented as a single-agent CrewAI Crew with one Task, to show what a framework's Agent/Task/Crew abstraction buys over a hand-rolled reason-act-observe loop: declarative role/goal/backstory framing and a different mental model for the same underlying action space
queue.description: Same tool-calling problem as example 1, rebuilt in CrewAI: one Agent with a role/goal/backstory, one Task wrapping the user request, the same tool set. Produces an equivalent typed result to id 1 so a learner can diff the two implementations line for line.
queue.synthetic_data: Reuse the same 2-3 sample inputs as id 1 verbatim so output can be diffed directly against the LangGraph and raw-loop versions.

queue.harness_focus: framework-comparison sibling of id 1 -- identical tool-calling task reimplemented as a pure LangChain Expression Language chain (RunnableSequence + bind_tools, no LangGraph state machine) to isolate what LangGraph's explicit graph/state model adds over LCEL's pipe-composition model for the same action space -- directly resolves the contrast flagged but never built in id 26's harness_focus
queue.description: Same tool-calling problem as example 1, rebuilt with pure LangChain Expression Language (no LangGraph): a RunnableSequence piping prompt -> model.bind_tools() -> tool-execution branch -> output parser. Same sample inputs and expected outputs as id 1.
queue.synthetic_data: Reuse the same 2-3 sample inputs as id 1.

queue.harness_focus: framework-comparison sibling of id 1 -- identical tool-calling task reimplemented using AutoGen/AG2's ConversableAgent + UserProxyAgent message-passing model instead of a single-process reason-act loop, to show conversation-as-control-flow (agents exchanging messages to drive execution) as a fourth distinct mental model alongside the raw loop (id 25), the graph (id 1), and the chain (id 101)
queue.description: Same tool-calling problem as example 1, rebuilt in AutoGen/AG2: a UserProxyAgent and an AssistantAgent exchange messages, with tool execution wired through AutoGen's function-calling registration, contrasting the message-passing control-flow model against the graph/chain/loop versions of the identical task.
queue.synthetic_data: Reuse the same 2-3 sample inputs as id 1.
